inputs.reasoning: Many alternations inside a Kleene star forces many kInstAlt jobs onto the BitState stack, potentially triggering GrowStack and exercising the main TrySearch loop extensively.
Multiple capture groups with star repetitions create kInstCapture and kInstAltMatch instructions, exercising the capture and byte-range handling in BitState.
Named captures with alternation exercises kInstAlt switch(arg) cases and kInstCapture paths in BitState.
Deep alternation with plus repetitions creates many Alt instructions, pushing njob_ toward maxjob_ to trigger GrowStack.
Nested groups with alternations and repetitions exercise CheckAndLoop path and ShouldVisit checks in BitState.
Chain of star repetitions generates kInstAltMatch instructions; greedy matching exercises the greedy branch of kInstAltMatch.
Alternation with progressively longer strings exercises kInstByteRange p<end check and ShouldVisit in the BitState engine.
Deeply nested alternations with counted repetition creates many Alt nodes, stressing the job stack and GrowStack path.
Nested optional groups create kInstCapture and kInstByteRange sequences, exercising p<end checks and capture register saving.
Alternation of character classes inside Kleene star generates many Alt and ByteRange instructions, pushing many jobs and potentially triggering GrowStack.
